## [00:00 - 03:01]

Hi, this is Don Krauser. Let's talk about your webinar registration page. So lately, there has been a trend to make webinar registration pages much more complicated with a lot of different elements than they really should be, which is herding conversions. Now, on the surface, it makes sense to add some of these elements, elements that pre-sell the webinar that should increase conversions, like and about the presenter section, like testimonials, like an additional description, like another block down below that also talks about what else you'll learn in the webinar. The problem is that these things that logically seem like they should help increase webinar sign up don't actually generally serve that purpose. They don't generally work, or if they do, they don't actually increase webinar attendance. So we might get a few more sign ups, but we don't get any more attendance. Our testing and experience shows that simpler is generally better when it comes to a webinar registration page. So let's talk about the elements that work. A headline describing what the webinar is, why they should attend it. A one to two sentence description of the webinar, three to five bullet points, what you'll discover in this webinar, the date and the time of the webinar, a sign up button, and then somewhere in there oftentimes as a pre-headline language that clearly indicates that they're registering for free webinar. Sometimes I'll encounter a webinar registration page that actually looks like they're opting in for something when they're signing up for a webinar. And that will definitely hurt your webinar attendance if you do not make it clear that they're signing up for a webinar. And the final thing is a privacy notice, usually four or five words, we respect your privacy, we will not sell your information, just small type just below the the sign up button. Now you don't need anything else on that page. Unfortunately, what has happened is this, a lot of the web page building software tools out there have tried to differentiate themselves from everybody else by making fancier looking pages. And one of the things they've started doing is putting a bunch of other things on the templates that you use to sign up for webinars, as well as some of the webinar companies themselves are adding these into their templates. And what we find is they don't actually work, they actually hurt your conversion and attendance. So you don't need anything else other than what I just showed you here. But let's talk about what sometimes works.

Now the key here is a couple of keys. First off, your registration page must be a standalone page. So there shouldn't be any navigation links on that page other than a privacy policy in terms of service. So you can't just take like a create a page on your blog that has those navigation elements on it and expect that page to perform well. Basically, there should be no other links taking taking them off of the page. The only options that they should have when they come to your webinar registration page is to register or to leave.
